prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `b71a7747`.
codex_response:
Implemented `b71a7747` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b71a7747/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b71a7747/verifier.py#L1), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b71a7747/generator.py#L1). The rule is: repeatedly peel away scaffold rows and columns whose palettes are contained in the current top-row palette until only the embedded two-color drawing remains. The Opus hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it as a starting hypothesis; `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` had no entry, so no [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b71a7747) was needed. No new shared primitive was added.

The generator builds the dense two-color output first, then embeds it into one of the observed scaffold families: a `19x19` three-color lattice, a `25x25` two-stage bordered scaffold, or a `28x28` two-color lattice. Validation is complete: `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task b71a7747` passed `3/3`, `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task b71a7747 --n-examples 1000` succeeded, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task b71a7747` passed `1000/1000`, and I opened [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b71a7747/previews/preview_01.png) to sanity-check the distribution.
